The big battle of Bengal saw a steep surge in the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) brigade, as it broke into Mamata Banerjee’s long-standing bastion and dented her towering political influence across the state. The saffron camp crossed the 200-seat mark, securing a record and decisive majority for the first time.The BJP’s rise to power in West Bengal brought an abrupt end to Mamata Banerjee’s 15-year rule in the state.West Bengal voted in two phases on April 23 and April 29, recording a historic voter turnout of over 90 percent. The polling exercise also came after the Election Commission’s special intensive revision process, adding to the intensity of the electoral battle.On counting day, May 4, the saffron wave visibly overtook the Trinamool Congress’s green surge, with gains reflecting both in vote share and seat margins. The BJP secured around 46 percent of the vote, while the TMC stood at approximately 40.8 percent.The Trinamool Congress, led by Mamata Banerjee, had come to power in 2011 after ending decades of Left Front rule in the state. However, in this election cycle, it was pushed to the margins as the BJP emerged as the dominant force, marking a historic political shift in West Bengal.
Article Summary: Repolling for the falta assembly constituency in west bengal has been scheduled for may 21. What worked for TVK?

Star power

‘DMK political enemy, BJP ideological foe’

Secularism pitch

Voter fatigue

Poll promises

NEW DELHI: Lights, camera, action -- and it’s a blockbuster.Tamil Nadu assembly poll results have propelled actor-turned-politician Thalapathy Vijay to the centre stage, with TVK emerging as the single largest party -- a dramatic debut that has upended the state's familiar political script.According to Election Commission trends, TVK is leading in 106 of the 234 seats. The AIADMK follows with 42 seats, while the DMK has 38. The BJP has secured 4 seats, the Congress 2, and others account for 8.For the first time in decades, Tamil Nadu’s long-standing two-party dominance appears to be breaking. What began as a fan-driven movement has now translated into a sweeping electoral mandate, dismantling the long-standing dominance of the DMK and AIADMK.In Tamil Nadu, cinema and politics go hand in hand. Film stars are often revered, and cinema does not merely entertain but also shapes public life.Vijay’s popularity could be a key factor behind TVK emerging as the single largest party. Lakhs attended his rallies -- either to hear him speak or catch a glimpse of him. The superstar enjoys support across classes, genders, age groups, and regions.He now finds himself in the company of M G Ramachandran and J Jayalalithaa, who transitioned from cinema to occupy the state’s top political office.While MGR served as chief minister for over a decade, Jayalalithaa held the post for more than 14 years across six terms.Yet, this immense popularity has not always translated into electoral success.Kamal Haasan entered politics in February 2018 with the launch of Makkal Needhi Maiam, projecting it as a centrist alternative to both DMK and AIADMK. However, the party failed to win any seats in the 2019 Lok Sabha elections.Rajinikanth had sparked massive excitement by announcing plans to launch a political party, but he later reversed his decision.Unlike many film stars, Vijay’s supporters are organised and politically active. In the 2021 local body polls, candidates backed by his fan clubs won 115 of the 169 seats they contested.His fan networks have also acted as ground-level workers and social media amplifiers, organising blood donation drives, medical camps, education support initiatives, and disaster relief efforts.TVK has also built a strong social media presence. Vijay has claimed that his party runs one of the largest digital volunteer networks in the country.“This is not just our claim - others have acknowledged it after seeing our reach. You are no longer just fans; you are TVK’s virtual warriors,” he had said.This strong urban and youth backing appears to have translated into electoral gains.In the run-up to the polls, Vijay outlined a clear political stance, calling the DMK his party’s political rival and the BJP its ideological adversary.He dismissed speculation about alliances, saying, “No one can paint TVK with a certain colour or call us a ‘B team’. Our principle is Pirapokkum ella uyirkum — all are equal by birth.”TVK positioned itself as an alternative for voters undecided between the DMK and the AIADMK-led NDA. It drew support from those unhappy with the DMK due to anti-incumbency, as well as voters wary of the NDA over issues such as the north-south divide, the three-language policy, and the delimitation debate.Anti-incumbency also played a role. The DMK government under chief minister MK Stalin faced criticism over corruption, unmet promises regarding jobs and women’s safety, and a perception of complacency.While the DMK emphasised the north-south divide in its campaign, Vijay avoided making it central. Instead, TVK focused on social justice and transparent governance, which appears to have resonated across urban and rural areas.His campaign blended welfare promises with anti-corruption messaging and youth outreach, drawing large crowds and leveraging his widespread appeal.During campaigning, Vijay stressed that his party’s ideology is rooted in “secular social justice,” asserting there would be “zero compromise on communal harmony.”He likened Tamil Nadu’s soil to “a mother’s heart, filled with love.”“Even if lifestyles and religions differ, aren’t we all brothers?” he said, adding that true faith fosters respect for others’ beliefs.The results also suggest voter fatigue with the long-standing dominance of Dravidian majors.For decades, politics in the state revolved around the DMK and AIADMK, both claiming the legacy of social justice and Tamil pride. Over time, sections of voters felt both had grown stagnant. The DMK faced corruption allegations, while the AIADMK weakened after the death of J Jayalalithaa.TVK stepped into this space with a fresh face and a popular leader. Vijay’s film career had already built a mass connect, allowing the party to bypass years of traditional cadre-building. It now appears poised to reshape the state’s political landscape.Attention is now turning to TVK’s poll promises, including eight grams of gold for brides, Rs 2,500 monthly assistance for women heads of households, and a pledge of “honest administration.”Though the funding of these welfare measures was debated ahead of the elections, the party has expressed confidence in implementing them. A party source said these initiatives would be prioritised if TVK forms the government.The party has proposed Rs 2,500 monthly assistance for women under 60 not covered under similar schemes, along with eight grams of gold and a silk saree for brides from low-income families.Other promises include creating five lakh government jobs, offering five lakh paid internships, a monthly stipend of Rs 4,000 for unemployed graduates, and education loans of up to Rs 20 lakh for higher studies.For voters in Tamil Nadu, this election was a turning point. A new party led by a hugely popular star has shown that fresh ideas can take on even the most established political forces. The gains made by Vijay’s TVK appear to have come directly at the DMK’s expense
Article Summary: Tamil nadu assembly poll results have propelled actor-turned-politician thalapathy vijay to the centre stage. Tvk emerging as the single largest party -- a dramatic debut that has upended the state's familiar political script. For the first time in decades, tamil nadu’s long-standing two-party dominance appears to be breaking.

NEW DELHI: West Bengal delivered a historic verdict on Monday as BJP is set to secure its first-ever assembly election victory in the state, ending chief minister Mamata Banerjee ’s 15-year rule and breaking the Trinamool Congress stronghold.The results come after a record voter turnout of 92.47%, reflecting a highly charged and closely watched election. The BJP’s campaign slogan "Bhoy out, Bharosa in" appeared to resonate with voters, overtaking the TMC’s long-standing "Maa maati manush" narrative.Counting of votes began at 8 am across 77 centres under tight security, covering 293 of the 294 Assembly seats. As trends firmed up through the day, the BJP showed a sharp surge compared to its 2021 performance, where it had won 77 seats with a 38.15% vote share.By midday, multiple trackers indicated the BJP either crossing or nearing the majority mark, with leads in the range of 140–150 seats and vote share estimates between 46% and 48%. The TMC, in contrast, lagged significantly behind in the trends.Polling was conducted in two phases on April 23 and April 29, with an electorate of more than 3.21 crore voters participating.As the final results take shape, the focus now shifts to the full list of BJP winners and losers across the constituencies, which will define the scale and impact of this landmark political shift in West Bengal.
Article Summary: Repolling for the falta assembly constituency in west bengal has been scheduled for may 21. Repolling will take place on may 21 and 22. The results will be announced on may 25.

NEW DELHI: RG Kar rape victim’s mother and BJP candidate Ratna Debnath is currently leading in the Panihati assembly constituency in North 24 Parganas.The Panihati seat has emerged as one of the most closely watched contests in the West Bengal election, with Debnath facing TMC candidate Tirthankar Ghosh, the son of a five-time sitting MLA.The constituency falls under the Dumdum Lok Sabha seat and includes wards 1–14, 16, 17 and 22 of Panihati Municipality.The battle in Panihati has drawn intense attention from both the BJP and the Trinamool Congress, with widespread campaigning seen across the constituency.Ratna Debnath has framed her candidature on her daughter’s memory and concerns over women’s safety. Debnath had earlier stated, "I chose to fight in this election for my daughter and for women whose security is under threat under the TMC government.Mamata Banerjee and her corrupt party leaders are responsible for my daughter's death. People welcome me when I reach out for votes and assure me of their support. I lost my daughter, but I want to serve the people and help them. I may not be a doctor like her, but as a representative, I want to continue her mission."Against this backdrop, Panihati has become a high-stakes constituency where concerns over governance, accountability and women’s safety intersect with established political loyalties.The result is being closely watched across West Bengal as counting progresses.Elsewhere in West Bengal, Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee is leading ahead of BJP candidate Suvendu Adhikari.According to Election Commission trends, the Bharatiya Janata Party is leading on 121 seats, the Trinamool Congress is ahead on 71 seats, and its ally BGPM is leading on one seat.West Bengal recorded the highest-ever voter turnout since independence with an impressive 91.66% polling in Phase-II of the Assembly elections. In the phase-I the poll-participation was 93.19%, taking the combined poll percentage to 92.47%.
Article Summary: Ratna debnath ahead as bjp candidate gains early lead in bengal counting. Panihati seat has emerged as one of the most closely watched contests in the west bengal election. The result is being closely watched across west bengal as counting progresses.
